title: Kiper's new NFL mock draft: How free agency shook up first-round landing spots for top prospects
content: Team needs have shifted in free agency, so how will top prospects come off the board in Round 1? Mel Kiper Jr. projects the NFL draft's first 32 picks.

title: Venezuela rallies past Italy to make first WBC final
content: Venezuela rallied from an early deficit to knock off Italy and set up a date with the U.S. in the country's first World Baseball Classic.

title: Bama preps to play without Holloway after arrest
content: Alabama coach Nate Oats said the team is preparing to play without star Aden Holloway in the NCAA tournament following Holloway's arrest Monday morning on a felony drug charge.

title: KD says loss to Lakers on him: 'I'm the offense'
content: The Lakers, in the process of carving out a defensive identity, threw a steady diet of double-teams at Kevin Durant in the fourth quarter Monday, leading to disastrous results for Houston. "I just felt like I lost the game for us tonight," said Durant.

title: Hawks fans rep Magic City despite nixed promo
content: Hawks fans showed up in Magic City gear Monday night even though the NBA shut down the team's short-lived promotion with the well-known strip club.

title: Ducks' Moore reveals 'heavy' depression battle
content: Oregon quarterback Dante Moore has revealed he struggled with mental health issues associated with the pressures of major college football.

title: Sources: Jets trade QB Fields to Chiefs for pick
content: The Jets have agreed to trade quarterback Justin Fields to the Chiefs, pending a physical, sources told ESPN's Adam Schefter on Monday.

title: Source: 49ers add Kirk to further boost WR spot
content: The 49ers and wide receiver Christian Kirk have agreed to a one-year contract, a source confirmed to ESPN.

content: We're following NFL free agency signings, trades, cuts, contract restructures and other moves as they happen.

content: Will any of the wild cards make a run? Plus, full playoff projections, draft lottery standings, more.

content: The NBA seems closer than ever to adding two franchises. Here's what we know, including how an expansion draft could work.

title: A pair of holes-in-one lead the best moments from TGL season
content: Neal Shipley and Tom Kim recorded the only holes-in-one in TGL history during the 2026 regular season.

title: Real or Not: These 8 MLB stars can reach new heights in 2026
content: Another record for Shohei Ohtani? A 40/40 season for Juan Soto? We hand out preseason Real or Not verdicts.

content: With the first matchday of the 2026 season in the books, ESPN ranks all 16 NWSL clubs from first to worst in this week's edition of Power Rankings.

title: Liverpool's most pivotal match of the season is up next -- and Slot's job may depend on it
content: As Liverpool continue to unravel, this week's Champions League fixture against Galatasaray is make-or-break for Slot's standing at the club.

content: Check out the results of our final fantasy baseball head-to-head points mock draft of 2026, featuring round-by-round picks and a snapshot of the rosters of all 10 teams.

content: Every week in fantasy basketball, there are available players who can help fill holes in your roster. Here are the ones to add for the week ahead.

title: MLS Power Rankings: LAFC off to a record-breaking start
content: LAFC have already made history with their lockdown defense, but are they No. 1? Here's this week's edition of ESPN's MLS Power Rankings.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Man United outline preferred midfield targets
content: Manchester United have outlined their summer transfer targets, while another European giant has entered the race for out-of-contract midfielder Leon Goretzka.

title: Ujjain Student Killed In Canada, Family Battles Grief, Rs 50 Lakh Burden
content: To bring Gurkirat's body back to India, the family has been asked to deposit nearly $40,000 around Rs 35 lakh. The hospital procedures will take another 15 days, and transporting the body to Ujjain would cost an additional Rs 10 lakh.

title: Trinamool's Latest Line-Up Has 2 Ministers Who Ran Into 'Messi' Problem
content: The Trinamool Congress has given tickets to contest the West Bengal assembly election to two leaders who ran into a controversy over an event featuring footballer Lionel Messi in December 2025.

title: Hormuz Escape Route Suggests Ships Navigating On Iran's Terms
content: Since US and Israeli attacks began a little over two weeks ago, Iran has hit several ships in and around the strait, and all but closed the waterway.

title: Opinion: Blog | West Bengal: BJP's Grand Plan Behind Fielding Suvendu Adhikari From 2 Seats
content: By turning the contest into a direct confrontation with Mamata Banerjee, the BJP aims to sharpen the narrative, energise its base, and raise the stakes of the election battle.

title: Is Mojtaba Khamenei Recovering At A Hospital In Moscow? What Russia Said
content: Mojtaba Khamenei, the new Supreme Leader of Iran, has reportedly been transferred to Russia for medical treatment after he was severely injured in the US-Israeli airstrike that killed his father on February 28.

title: After Delhi Bridge Collapse Kills Woman, Government Orders Citywide Audit
content: Irrigation and Flood Control Minister Parvesh Verma has ordered an immediate investigation into the collapse of a foot overbridge in north Delhi's Roop Nagar on Tuesday morning.

title: Senior Assam Leader Threatens To Quit Congress Over Poll Nomination
content: Senior Congress leader and Lok Sabha MP Pradyut Bordoloi has warned the party leadership that he may resign from the Congress if Lahorighat MLA Asif Mohammad Nazar is renominated for the upcoming Assam Assembly elections.

title: Trump's Delay Of Summit With Xi Jinping Comes As A Win For China
content: US President Donald Trump's request for a delay to his summit with Chinese leader Xi Jinping is a likely welcome development for Beijing, even as it threatens to inject new uncertainty into ties between the world's two largest economies.

title: Trinamool Announces Candidates For 2026 Bengal Polls: Check Full List
content: The Trinamool Congress has released its candidate list for the 2026 West Bengal Assembly Election. Party chief and Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee announced that the party will contest 291 out of 294 seats.

title: History Repeats Itself In Nandigram Where Suvendu Adhikari Faces His Ex-Aide
content: A top leader versus a former aide -- this was the script that played out in Bengal's Nandigram in the last assembly election, with dramatic results. This time, it is about a see a reprise, with an aide of the sitting MLA -- the BJP's Suvendu Adhikari

title: Trump Claims Surprise Over Iran Retaliation Against Gulf Allies Despite Warning
content: President Donald Trump was warned that attacking Iran could trigger retaliation against US Gulf allies despite his claims on Monday that Tehran's reaction came as a surprise, said a US official and two sources familiar with US intelligence reports.

title: Exiled Iran Prince Reza Pahlavi Backs Noble Prize Winner To Lead Justice Panel
content: The exiled son of Iran's last shah announced a new committee on Monday to lay the groundwork for a future truth commission in Iran and named Nobel laureate Shirin Ebadi to lead it.

title: Emirates Flies Almost Empty Jets To Dubai As War Scares Off Travellers
content: Emirates is operating flights to Dubai that are near-empty in some cases as travellers avoid the Persian Gulf, highlighting the challenges for the world's largest international airline to restore its network amid a protracted war.

title: Man Created AI Reel With Nitish Kumar's Son And MLA Maithili Thakur, Arrested
content: A man in Bihar's Gaya, which is globally renowned as a spiritual destination, has been arrested for using artificial intelligence to make an objectionable reel involving Chief Minister Nitish Kumar's son, Nishant, and first-time MLA Maithili Thakur.

title: Ground Report: NDTV Visits Cafe Where Netanyahu Shot Video After Death Rumours
content: A cafe in Israel's Jerusalem has become a subject of intense speculation on social media in connection with the whereabouts of Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.

title: Mojtaba Rejects Ceasefire Push, 'Very Serious' About Revenge On US, Israel: Report
content: Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei has rejected proposals for reducing tensions or for a possible ceasefire with the United States that were conveyed to Tehran by two intermediary countries.

title: Opinion: In Numbers: The Curious Reason Iran Eased Down Its 'Drone Swarms' After March 9
content: Based on the data compiled, we can bucket the strike pattern broadly into three distinct phases: an initial 'saturation' attack, a sustained drone attrition campaign, and a sudden decline in attack intensity.

title: First Batch Of Indians Back, Many Still Stuck As Azerbaijan Shuts Iran Border
content: As the war broke out on February 28 with massive bombing on Tehran, borders were closed, flights were suspended and communications were severely hit.

title: Iran War Live Updates: Israel Says It Has Killed Top Iranian Security Chief Ali Larijani, Senior Commander Gholamreza Soleimani
content: US-Israel-Iran War News LIVE Updates: The war in the Middle East broke out when the US and Israel attacked Iran on February 28.

title: Rahul Gandhi's Conduct Inappropriate In Parliament, Say 200 Ex-Officials
content: Hundreds of retired bureaucrats and former military officers have accused Leader of Opposition Rahul Gandhi of undermining the dignity and decorum of parliament and demanded an apology from the Congress leader.

title: 'Iran posed no threat': Trump's counterterror chief resigns, cites Israel 'pressure'
content: A top counterterrorism official, Joe Kent, resigned, stating Iran posed no imminent threat and the US-Iran conflict was driven by Israeli pressure and misinformation. Kent, a veteran and Gold Star husband, cited his personal losses and the deceptive tactics used to push for war, echoing the Iraq War's origins. He urged President Trump to reconsider the current path.

title: Popular analyst says that layoffs at Zuckerberg's Meta could trigger a 'wave of panic'
content: Meta's potential workforce reduction of up to 20% is viewed by analysts not as a setback, but as a strategic move to become an AI-first organization. This restructuring aims to leverage AI for significant cost and performance advantages, potentially creating an insurmountable competitive edge. The company's substantial AI investments and previous successes in efficiency suggest this could redefine industry standards.

title: Iran vs US tensions cloud FIFA World Cup plans, matches may shift to Mexico
content: Iran’s 2026 World Cup participation is uncertain amid rising tensions. The country is negotiating with FIFA to shift matches from the US to Mexico, citing safety concerns. Officials said, "We will certainly not travel to America." FIFA hasn’t agreed yet. After Donald Trump warned about safety, Iran insisted "no one can exclude" them from the tournament.

title: Exclusive - 'Action will follow. Pak's brutality won't go unanswered': Taliban
content: Taliban spokesperson Zabihullah Mujahid vowed retaliation after an alleged Pakistani airstrike on Omar Addiction Treatment Hospital in Kabul, which he described as a deliberate attack on civilians. Mujahid stated the attack "will not go unanswered" and accused Pakistan of attempting to shape the narrative, vowing to expose their "brutality" to global human rights bodies.

title: AB de Villiers tells MS Dhoni 'bat higher or be out of CSK squad'
content: Dhoni, 44, will play for CSK when the season begins on March 28. De Villiers said Dhoni’s recent trend of batting at No. 8 or 9 limits his impact.

title: Nvidia CEO seemingly 'realises' that Google, Microsoft and Meta are set to eat company's lunch
content: Nvidia's dominance in AI hardware is facing a significant challenge as customers seek cheaper, purpose-built inference chips.  Major tech companies like Google, Microsoft, and Meta are developing their own alternatives, directly competing with Nvidia's established GPU dominance. This shift signals the end of Jensen Huang's 'one chip fits all' era.

title: 'We just wanted talks': Sonam Wangchuk's first reaction after release from jail
content: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk, released after NSA detention, stated Ladakh's goal is dialogue with the Centre. He called his release a "win-win," enabling trust-building for meaningful talks. Wangchuk, detained for six months, expressed relief at avoiding sharing his "horror stories" from jail, hoping for constructive engagement.

title: LPG crisis hits IT firms; canteens cut, staff asked to bring lunch
content: A cooking gas crunch, stemming from the West Asia conflict, has forced major Indian IT firms like Infosys, TCS, and Cognizant to drastically cut canteen services. Employees are now compelled to bring packed lunches, impacting thousands who rely on office cafeterias, especially those in PGs and hostels with limited options.

title: January meeting led Sam Altman to pause OpenAI ‘adult mode’ after warnings
content: OpenAI has delayed its proposed "adult mode" for ChatGPT following internal concerns about user safety, including emotional dependence and access by minors. Advisers warned of potential misuse and unhealthy user behavior, prompting the company to re-evaluate its age-detection systems and content boundaries before a future release.

title: RS polls: Cong alleges BJP tried ‘vote chori’ in Haryana, warns cross-voters of strict action
content: The Congress accused the BJP of "vote chori" and "murdering democracy" in Haryana's Rajya Sabha elections after cross-voting and invalid ballots marred the contest. Despite acknowledging internal cross-voting and promising strict action against its MLAs, the Congress secured one seat, while the BJP won the other. The outcome has ignited a political battle and an internal party crisis.

title: Smith, Warner’s PSL plans under scanner amid ‘do not travel’ alert
content: Steve Smith and David Warner face uncertainty over joining the Pakistan Super League due to rising security concerns. Tensions, travel disruptions, and a “Do Not Travel” alert for Peshawar have raised alarms. While preparations continue, players are weighing safety risks, with contingency plans like emergency evacuation under consideration before final decisions are made.

title: Why Trump's 'beautiful armada' cannot take control of the Strait of Hormuz
content: The proposal from President Trump to assemble an international naval coalition for the defense of the Strait of Hormuz encounters formidable obstacles. Iran's advanced A2/AD capabilities—featuring an array of missiles, drones, and submarines—combined with the strait's challenging topography, complicates even the strongest navy's efforts to maintain a permanent presence.

title: RCB’s Hazlewood to miss IPL 2026 start, out for two weeks
content: Josh Hazlewood will miss the start of IPL 2026 due to injury, delaying his return by nearly five months. Pat Cummins is also recovering and may join later. Meanwhile, players like Steve Smith face safety concerns over joining the Pakistan Super League amid regional tensions, with evacuation plans being considered.

title: India slams Pak for ‘fabricating’ Islamophobia narratives; flags Ahmadiyya repression, Afg bombings
content: India has accused Pakistan of fabricating Islamophobia narratives at the UN, questioning Islamabad's treatment of minorities and its actions against Afghanistan. Ambassador Parvathaneni Harish urged the UN to address all religious discrimination, not just one faith, highlighting India's large Muslim population and pluralistic ethos. He warned against weaponizing religion for political gain.

title: Exclusive - 'If I could change my body, I would': Sumit Nagal on injuries
content: India's Sumit Nagal is fighting back after a turbulent career plagued by injuries, including hip surgery and a hip flexor tear, that saw him drop from World No. 68 to No. 278. Now refocused on fitness, diet, and the controllables of his game, Nagal heads into his favourite time of the season - on clay - with hard-won maturity and renewed determination.

title: US-Iran war: How LPG cylinder has become the face of Middle East mayhem's impact on India
content: Global tensions in the Middle East are directly impacting Indian households, causing a severe LPG shortage.  Rising fuel costs and disrupted shipments have led to panic bookings, long queues, and black marketing, forcing businesses to adapt and highlighting India's heavy reliance on imports.  The situation is escalating, affecting daily life and livelihoods across the nation.

title: Salman Agha runout: Should umpires have called dead ball? MCC settles debate
content: The Marylebone Cricket Club (MCC) has confirmed a controversial run-out in the Bangladesh vs. Pakistan ODI was correctly given out under the Laws of Cricket. The non-striker was out of his ground when the wicket was broken, and the ball remained in play, making the dismissal lawful.

title: Govt steps in, instructs CBFC to take strict action against Nora Fatehi's song 'Sarke Chunar'
content: A controversial song from 'KD: The Devil', featuring Nora Fatehi and Sanjay Dutt, has drawn government intervention. Amidst allegations of vulgar lyrics and visuals, the Hindi version was removed from YouTube.

title: 'Use LPG wisely': Centre says supply 'matter of concern'; advises consumers not to panic
content: India's energy crisis persists due to Middle East conflict, but the government assures consumers about LPG supplies. While urging judicious consumption and alternate fuel use, authorities are conducting raids to curb hoarding and black marketing of LPG cylinders across several states.

title: US gas prices: Hormuz tensions push diesel above $5/gallon, highest since Dec 2022
content: Retail diesel prices in the U.S. have surpassed $5 a gallon for the second time, impacting global economic activity and consumer goods. This surge, driven by the US-Israeli conflict with Iran and disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, presents a challenge for the upcoming midterm elections.  Gasoline prices also reached their highest since October 2023, with little sign of easing.

title: When is Eid-ul-Fitr 2026? Key dates, historical context and significance explained
content: Eid-ul-Fitr in India is anticipated on March 20 or 21, 2026, contingent on the moon sighting. This joyous festival marks the end of Ramadan's fasting and spiritual reflection.  It's a time for gratitude, community, and renewed faith, celebrating the successful completion of a month of discipline and devotion.

title: SC rules adoptive mothers can’t be denied maternity leave if child is older than 3 months
content: The Supreme Court has struck down a provision in the Social Security Code 2020 that limited maternity leave for adoptive mothers. The court declared the provision irrational, stating adoptive children are no different from biological ones. Consequently, adoptive mothers are now entitled to 12 weeks of maternity leave, irrespective of the child's age at adoption.

title: 'We were shocked': Trump reacts to Iran hitting US Gulf allies — was there prior warning?
content: US President Trump expressed shock at Iran's expanded strikes across the Middle East, despite US intelligence anticipating such retaliation. The conflict, now in its third week, has seen Iranian attacks on US positions and allied infrastructure, disrupting vital shipping lanes like the Strait of Hormuz.  Concerns are mounting over the war's justification and its escalating regional impact.

title: 5 IPL legends to have not won the Orange Cap
content: As IPL 2026 nears, a surprising fact emerges: several top run-scorers have never won the Orange Cap. Legends like Rohit Sharma, Shikhar Dhawan, Suresh Raina, AB de Villiers, Ajinkya Rahane, and MS Dhoni, despite their high rankings and impactful careers, have surprisingly never finished a season as the leading run-scorer.

title: Jawahar Navodaya Class 6 result out: Direct scorecard link here
content: The Navodaya Vidyalaya Samiti has released the JNVST Class 6 Result 2026 for summer-bound Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalayas. Students who appeared in the Phase 1 entrance exam held in December 2025 can now check their qualifying status online. The selection list marks the next stage of the admission process, with shortlisted candidates required to complete document verification.

title: IPL 2026: KL Rahul to lead Delhi Capitals charge from the top
content: KL Rahul is set to open for the Delhi Capitals in IPL 2026. The move follows a mid-season shift that boosted his form. The 33-year-old will also keep wickets for the Delhi Capitals in this season. With a strong squad featuring experienced Indian players and impactful overseas signings, Capitals aim to bounce back and push for their maiden IPL title.

title: Gold price today: Check the price of 18K, 22K and 24K gold in your city
content: Gold prices surged on Tuesday, with April 2026 futures on MCX rising 1% to Rs 1,56,996 per 10 grams. Investors sought safe-haven assets amid escalating Middle East tensions involving the US, Israel, and Iran, which also kept oil prices above $100 per barrel. Globally, spot gold edged up slightly.

title: Watch: After Shivalik, Nanda Devi LPG tanker reaches India
content: The LPG tanker Nanda Devi arrived in Gujarat carrying 46,500 metric tonnes of LPG, set for ship-to-ship transfer. This marks the second successful LPG carrier navigating the Strait of Hormuz amid global tensions. The shipment will help manage India's LPG supply pressures.

title: ‘Kaise kheloge?’: Wife’s honest question that sparked Suryakumar’s India career
content: Suryakumar Yadav credits his wife Devisha's "brutally honest" question in 2018 as the catalyst for his international cricket career.  Her direct inquiry about his aspirations pushed him to implement disciplined lifestyle changes, leading to consistent performances and his eventual India debut. Devisha's support extended beyond cricket, focusing on mindset and humility.

title: 'We don’t need anybody': Trump asks allies to help with Hormuz crisis amid Iran war; gets snubbed
content: US President Trump expressed disappointment as key allies like Germany and Italy refused to send warships to the Strait of Hormuz. Citing legal limits and lack of mandates, these nations declined. Trump, however, asserted US strength, suggesting the request was partly a test of allied commitment amid escalating regional conflict and Iran's closure of the vital waterway.

title: ‘Khoon garam tha’: Surya on how India’s 2026 T20 WC team differed from 2024
content: Captain Suryakumar Yadav distinguished India's T20 World Cup victories, noting the 2024 team's "experienced enthusiasm" and the 2026 squad's "fiery passion of youth." While both teams possessed skill, the 2024 side leveraged seasoned players like Rohit and Kohli, whereas the 2026 unit thrived on youthful intensity and hunger, especially winning on home soil.

title: Sunil Gavaskar faces ‘vile stuff’ attack from Pak-born player over Abrar comment
content: A controversy ignited after Sunil Gavaskar suggested Indian-owned franchises shouldn't sign Pakistani players, citing potential indirect financial contributions to India's adversaries. Pakistan-born England cricketer Azeem Rafiq fiercely condemned Gavaskar's remarks as "vile stuff." Franchise coaches defended player selections based on cricketing merit, while the BCCI stated no jurisdiction over foreign league decisions.

title: Can Iran's Russian made 'S-300' hunt Israel's 'Blue Sparrow' amid raging air battle across Gulf
content: The Russian-made S-300, first deployed in the late 1970s, is a long-range surface-to-air missile system designed to intercept aircraft, drones and incoming cruise or ballistic missiles. Over decades, it has earned a reputation as a robust shield, used by countries from Russia and Ukraine to Iran and Nato members like Greece. Its architecture relies on powerful radars such as the 30N6E2 "Tombstone" to track targets and guide interceptor missiles with high precision.

title: 'Time doesn't speak, but it waits': Rajinikanth reacts to TVK's 'DMK threat' claim
content: TVK functionary Aadhav Arjuna had alleged that the DMK family threatened actor Rajinikanth when he tried to enter politics. ​​Arjuna said he was not criticising Rajinikanth but added that Vijay had the courage to withstand such pressure. TVK organised protests at district headquarters across Tamil Nadu on Thursday, accusing the state government of failing to maintain law and order.

title: IPL 2026: Why SRH chose Ishan Kishan over Abhishek Sharma as captain
content: Ishan Kishan’s rise as a leadership contender stems from his strong domestic captaincy record, ability to manage young players, and tactical awareness as a wicketkeeper. His recent international form, successfully leading Jharkhand to the Syed Mushtaq Ali Trophy title, has strengthened his case, helping him get the vote of approval from the SRH management.

title: Old footbridge collapses in Delhi’s Roop Nagar; 1 dead
content: A woman died after falling into a drain when an old iron footbridge collapsed in north Delhi’s Roop Nagar, police said.

content: a back-three system can be among the most attacking and aggressive in the sport . the last decade or so has been littered with examples of how a team playing a . in the 20 premier league games he managed this season, the Red Devils registered the most shots on target (109) .

title: MMA divisional rankings: Vallejos punches his ticket into featherweight top 10
content: the 24-year-old featherweight has fought four times in the 145-pound division . he has scored exhilarating knockouts in three of the wins . his latest was the first-round TKO of Josh Emmett .

title: Spring buzz: Bama expectations, Texas upgrades and a freshman QB making waves
content: Alabama coach Kalen DeBoer says it's more than his 20-8 record in the past two seasons there . "we're going to do things the right way," he says in a sit-down interview . he is looking for his first college football playoff appearance with the Trojans .

title: Week 5: Top 25 rankings, top moments and what to watch
content: the SEC got off to a fast and hot start, with Arkansas taking its home series against Mississippi State in Fayetteville and Texas finally losing its first game of the season . the Tigers dropped two games on the road to Vanderbilt . this week's biggest riser is crosstown rival USC, which rose up 12 spots .

content: a late-monday airstrike at a drug rehabilitation hospital in Kabul, Afghanistan . the ministry of External Affairs condemns the strikes as a military operation . a blatant assault on Afghanistan’s sovereignty and a direct threat to regional peace and stability .

title: Iran wants WC games moved from U.S. to Mexico
content: Iran's embassy in Mexico says the country is negotiating with FIFA to move matches from the U.S. to Mexico . the embassy posted a statement attributed to Iranian soccer federation president Mehdi Taj . it was unclear whether such talks are happening with the country .

title: Ultimate season preview: Predictions, inside intel and breakout stars for all 30 teams
content: the 2026 season is less than two weeks away, with the new york Yankees visiting the San Francisco Giants in a March 25 season opener followed by a full schedule of opening day games the next day . here is a team-by-team look at the year ahead for all 30 MLB teams .

title: Jay Bilas predicts every game of the 2026 men's NCAA tournament
content: the 2026 men's NCAA tournament is revealed in a bracket . the bracket gives us less than five minutes . my bracket was chalky last season, but the backlash was swift and unrelenting . I was criticized for not picking an underdog to reach Sao Paulo .

title: What to watch in the semifinals
content: the second TGL season is coming to an end with the start of the soFi Cup playoffs . Boston Common will try to complete a worst-to-first season when it takes on Jupiter Links Golf Club in the other semifinal . the winners of Tuesday's matches will compete in a best-of-three finals series on March 23-24 .

title: Green galore: Revisiting NBA St. Patrick's Day uniforms of the past
content: a select group of teams each year would wear special green uniforms on St. Patrick's Day . the initiative ended when Nike became the NBA's uniform supplier ahead of the 2017-18 season . instead of one-off holiday uniforms, the NBA and WNBA agreed to a 12-year contract extension .

title: Odds: United States favored to beat Venezuela in final
content: The  World Baseball Classic  final is set, and Team USA is a sizable favorite to take home its second title Tuesday, while Venezuela will need to overcome underdog odds once again to win its first. The Americans are -285 money-line favorites over the Venezuelans, which sports +225 odds for the WBC's championship game. The U.S. had opened the tournament as the odds-on -110 favorite, while Venezuela began at +900. Team USA had attracted over 50% of the handle to win the WBC title going into the quarterfinals at BetMGM, making the Americans the sportsbook's largest liability. Venezuela, meanwhile, garnered just 2.4% of the handle, fifth-most at the book. On the tournament MVP front, Americans  Aaron Judge  (+370),  Roman Anthony  (+600) and  Bobby Witt Jr . (+850) top the odds board, along with Venezuelan  Ronald Acuna Jr.  (+750).  Maikel Garcia  (18-1) and  Luis Arraez  (20-1) are also in the running from Venezuela. For latest odds go to  DraftKings Sportsbook

title: Women's March Madness 2026 bracket: First look at all 68 teams
content: sixty-eight teams will compete in the 2026 women's NCAA tournament . the bracket revealed which teams are in, which bubble teams got left out and which matchups might be waiting on the road to Phoenix, where the national champion will be crowned on April 5 .

title: Women's tournament odds: UConn odds-on favorite; UCLA, Texas, South Carolina next
content: the shortest odds for a pre-tournament favorite since 2018 was also the last time UConn was favored going into the Big Dance . the SEC dominates the next four spots between Texas (+700), south Carolina (+800), LSU (16-1, opened 14-1) and Vanderbilt (50-1)

title: New Hindu temple in Dallas sparks backlash
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. With an unwavering commitment to accuracy, depth, and timeliness, we strive to keep you informed about the ever-evolving world, delivering a nuanced understanding of international affairs to our readers. Join us on a journey across continents as we unravel the stories that shape our interconnected world. Read More

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: Former KPCC general secretary M.J. Job joins BJP
content: He had quit the party over its decision to support former CPI(M) leader G. Sudhakaran in the Ambalapuzha constituency

title: AIR Vijayawada to broadcast special literary programme for Ugadi
content: The 59-minute programme will be broadcast on March 19 at 9.30 a.m. from the Vijayawada station and will be repeated at 10.30 a.m. on FM Rainbow 102.2., says programme head

title: No evidence of increased efficiency due to mechanisation of sewer cleaning, Centre tells LS
content: Centre says 622 deaths from hazardous cleaning reported since 2017, 842 complaints in 2025 over denial of wages, no protective gear, and caste bias; insists that sewer and septic tank cleaning work is “occupation-based”, not caste-based

title: Bengaluru receives first pre-monsoon showers
content: IMD officials said that the city will continue to receive light rainfall for the next two to three days

title: ECI deploys over 1,000 Central observers for upcoming Assembly polls
content: West Bengal has been assigned highest number—one general observer for each of 294 constituencies, along with 84 police observers and 100 expenditure observers.

title: Police intensify probe into Kowdiar Palace theft
content: Investigators compile list of all visitors who entered the palace between October and November, last year; they suspect the theft was carried out by someone familiar with the palace layout

title: A.P. BJP chief urges government to allocate funds for major festivals
content: Party has launched a special initiative titled ‘Janata Varadhi’ to strengthen coordination between the public and the government, says P.V.N. Madhav

title: West Asia conflict: Sharp drop in flight services to Dubai
content: Following recurring UAE airspace restrictions, triggered by continued retaliatory strikes by Iran on US bases in the Gulf

title: ‘Koil Alwar Thirumanjanam’ performed in Tirumala ahead of Ugadi
content: The traditional temple cleansing ritual has been performed in strict adherence to the Agama scriptures

title: A.P. govt. announces one-time 50% interest waiver on property tax arrears
content: Taxpayers can avail of the benefit by paying 100% of principal property tax dues along with 50% of interest in a single lump sum payment until March 31, 2026

title: Rayalaseema leaders seek immediate water release from Srisailam Dam
content: Submitting a representation to district Collector Rajakumari; emphasise that decisions on water release must align with existing GOs, Bachawat Tribunal provisions, and the Andhra Pradesh Reorganisation Act

title: Free travel scheme for differently-abled persons to be launched in A.P. on March 18
content: People with 40% or more disability can travel free of cost in APSRTC services, including city ordinary, metro express, Palle Velugu, Ultra-Palle Velugu, and express buses

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: BJP suspends dissident leader in Kottayam
content: Noble Mathew, national vice-president of Minority Morcha, has been suspended from the  primary membership of the party citing ‘anti-organisational activities’

title: Govt. sets May 15 as deadline for completion of all irrigation O&M works in Andhra Pradesh
content: Basic maintenance measures such as greasing equipment too have been ignored by the YSRCP government, alleges Water Resources Minister Nimmala Ramanaidu, and directs officials to undertake the works before the commencement of the 2026 Kharif season

title: Vijay, TVK seniors eye Chennai constituencies
content: Vijay is highly likely to make his electoral debut from the Perambur constituency, sources in the party say

title: Bengal polls: Mamata vs Suvendu once again; stage set for Round 2 of political rivalry

title: 39,349 students to appear for SSLC exam in Mysuru district
content: All necessary arrangements have been made at 136 examination centres, including 43 centres in Mysuru city alone

title: RDSS extends financial aid to families of 17 farmer suicide victims in Vijayawada
content: Each family given ₹50,000 to help rebuild their lives through small income-generating activities such as dairy farming, livestock rearing, or tailoring, says RDSS secretary

title: 14 arrested in Varanasi on BJP Yuva Morcha's complaint after hosting Iftar on Ganga
content: Police said that a video went viral on March 16, purportedly showing a group of people holding an Iftar party on a boat on the Ganga

title: Creating 20 lakh jobs is the government’s single-point agenda, says HRD Minister Lokesh
content: Urging the officials to ensure that the investment agreements translate into tangible projects, he says MoUs alone are not enough, and they must lead to real investments and employment generation

title: Bhim Army youth wing chief booked for offensive posts
content: The police have registered a suo motu case against Santosh Pala for hurting religious sentiments through offensive posts on social media

title: No immediate LPG shortage in Kerala, assures State government
content: Officials say that the supply lines remain stable and there is no immediate cause for concern; a multi-tiered monitoring system will oversee the overall LPG supply situation and guide allocation strategies in the State

title: ₹610 crore remains to be paid to Tamil Nadu under wage component of MGNREGS, Centre tells Lok Sabha
content: On January 23, while moving a motion on MGNREGS in the Assembly, Chief Minister M.K. Stalin said the figures of liability to Tamil Nadu were ₹1,026 crore (wage) and ₹1,087 crore (component)

title: Truck driver killed on the spot, six injured in road accident
content: A tanker carrying milk, a truck and a motorcycle were involved in the road accident near Hanchinal camp in Sindhanur taluk

title: No LPG shortage in Andhra Pradesh, says CM Naidu
content: Highlighting that the State has sufficient reserves to meet the demand for the next 15 days, the Chief Minister orders strict monitoring of LPG distribution through e-KYC and OTP-based systems to curb the diversion of cylinders

title: RSS announces major structural reorganisation to boost nationwide activities
content: To accelerate the expansion of its activities, the current framework consisting of 11 kshetras and 46 prants will be transitioned into a more granular system of nine kshetras and 85 sambhags

title: New Belagavi Mayor, Deputy Mayor to be chosen today
content: The BJP, which is sure to win both the posts, has issued a whip to all its members to be present for the elections expected to be unanimous

title: Jagan sees ‘widespread public discontent’ against NDA government in Andhra Pradesh
content: People are questioning why welfare schemes implemented earlier are no longer being continued, says the YSRCP chief and hopes that the party will return to power

title: DC urges students to imbibe Kannada poet Pu.Ti.Na.’s values
content: Despite being an agrarian district, Mandya’s literary contributions are notable, says Deputy Commissioner at the 121st birth anniversary of noted Kannada poet Purohit Thirunarayana Narasimhachar

title: Patch up for greater good, Assam citizens’ collective tells Congress, Raijor Dal
content: More than 170 intellectuals, social activists, and professionals said the deadlock between the two parties over ‘one or two constituencies’ could defeat the goal of providing a democratic alternative

title: BJP members allege rampant corruption in building plan approvals, issuing e-khatas in Bengaluru
content: BJP members representing Bengaluru alleged rampant corruption in building plan approvals, issuance of e-khatas, construction of retaining walls along storm-water drains (rajakaluves), and property tax collection by civic bodies under the Greater Bengaluru Authority

title: Fire at Thiruvananthapuram Medical College Hospital; none hurt
content: Blaze broke out after a ventilator in the surgical ICU burst; swift action by healthcare personnel averted major mishap; patients evacuated and moved to safety

title: DSS favours deferring internal quota until issues involved are resolved
content: The Dalit Sangharsh Samiti has urged the State government to continue with the earlier reservation system to avoid disruptions in recruitment

title: Sunil Bose seeks revival of stalled railway projects of Chamarajanagar
content: The MP urges the government to strengthen the railway network in the region for the benefit of the common people

title: No govt job through employment offices in five years in Rajasthan; 22 lakh candidates registered, says RTI
content: The data, provided by the Directorate of Employment, shows that as of January 14, a total of 22,21,317 candidates were registered as job seekers in district employment offices across the State; of these, over 13.08 lakh were male, 9.12 lakh female, and 989 fell under the 'other' category.

title: ARAI officials visit site in Mandya to set up proposed testing centre
content: The three-member team is expected to submit its report in 20 days after which a technical team will visit the land for further evaluation

title: Parliamentary panel rebukes Ministry of Planning, Niti Aayog for poor financial management
content: Standing Committee on Finance said the Ministry and Niti Aayog sought higher budgets each year, despite significantly under-utilising their funds

title: Samiti to organise Amar Balidan Divas in Varanasi on March 23
content: Jai Bharat Mata Seva Samiti will take out a procession from Lad Chincholi to commemorate the martyrdom of Bhagat Singh, Sukhdev and Rajguru

title: Artist documents A.P.’s artistic legacy in handbook
content: Yellapu Kalasagar’s Art of Andhra Pradesh brings together the works of 300 artists, capturing the diversity, tradition, and evolving spirit of art from the region

title: Shivamogga DC instructs officials to take measures to avoid KFD infections
content: The DC directs health officials to maintain strict surveillance in the Malnad region, particularly in ‘hotspots’ where monkey deaths have been reported

title: 1.68 lakh cases of defacement removed in Tamil Nadu, says Election Commission
content: It says the statues of late leaders need not be covered, but the photographs of politically active leaders must be covered. It also issues a clarification in respect of cases wherein individuals are  found carrying cash

title: Hyderabad Metro Rail to explore battery-operated pod network for last-mile access
content: Pilot proposed along the Raidurg-Kukatpally stretch, linking metro stations with nearby residential areas using 4-6 seater pods with a capacity of up to 10,000 passengers per hour

title: Hoysala team saves bike taxi rider from ending his life
content: Head Constable Sridhar Murthy found the man lying on the track after having allegedly consumed alcohol. He quickly pulled him to safety, preventing a potential tragedy.

title: Malayalam singers pay 10-ft tribute to SPB
content: The sculpture is being installed in the compound of the V.T. Bhattathiripad Cultural Complex near Government Medical College, Palakkad

title: Kodagu: No shortage of domestic LPG
content: The administration has warned of strict legal action against hoarding or illegal sale of LPG cylinders under relevant laws, including the Essential Commodities Act

title: ‘Many hotels, restaurants may soon shutdown in Mysuru if LPG supply is not restored immediately’
content: The situation has turned critical, as distributors have failed to supply the required LPG despite repeated appeals, says Hotel Owners’ Association president Narayana Gowda
